evals.prompt: What's the first step to making a new astrodb?
evals.expected_output: Explains that the first step is to create your own GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (via 'Use this template' on https://github.com/astrodbtoolkit/astrodb-template-db) and provide its URL, and asks for a name for the database — suggesting the user reuse that name for the new repo too. Does not ask for or mention a data file — that comes in a later step.

assertions.text: Instructs the user to create a new GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (e.g. 'Use this template' on astrodb-template-db) and to provide the new repo's URL
Asks the user for a database name
Suggests or encourages using the same name for the GitHub repo as for the database
Does not ask for or reference a data file, parsing, or ingestion (that is a later step)

evals.prompt: I made my repo from the template, it's at https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db . Call the database BdSurvey and get it set up.
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db right away, without first asking where to put it or what working directory/folder to use, verifies the cloned repo has the template structure (a data/ directory, a database.toml, and a schema.yaml), updates database.toml so db_name is 'BdSurvey', and in the final summary tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for next steps. Does not handle a data file.

assertions.text: Clones the provided repo URL (https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db)
Does not ask the user where to clone the repo or what directory/working directory to use before cloning
Verifies the cloned repo has the template directory structure: a data/ directory, a database.toml file, and a schema.yaml file
Updates database.toml so db_name is set to the user-provided name (BdSurvey)
In the wrap-up, tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for the next steps
Does not ask for or handle a data file

evals.prompt: Set up a new astrodb database called NearbyGalaxies.
evals.expected_output: Notes the database name (NearbyGalaxies) and, because no repo URL was given, asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template ('Use this template') and provide its URL before cloning — suggesting the new repo be named NearbyGalaxies (or something close) to match the database. Does not invent a repo URL or fabricate a clone.

assertions.text: Asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template and provide its URL before cloning
Suggests naming the new GitHub repo NearbyGalaxies, or something close to it, to match the database name
Does not invent a repo URL or clone an arbitrary repository
Does not ask for or reference a data file

evals.prompt: I want to start a new astrodb for my study of M dwarf flares. Let's call the database MdwarfFlares.
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the database name (MdwarfFlares), suggests the user give their new GitHub repo the same (or a closely matching) name, and walks them through creating a repo from the astrodb-template and sending its URL — without inventing a URL, cloning anything, or asking about a data file yet.

assertions.text: Acknowledges or confirms the database will be named MdwarfFlares
Suggests or recommends naming the new GitHub repo the same as, or closely matching, MdwarfFlares
Explains how to create a repo from the astrodb-template (e.g. 'Use this template' on astrodb-template-db) and asks for its URL
Does not invent a repo URL or attempt to clone anything
Does not ask for or reference a data file

evals.prompt: Here's my repo for a new astrodb: https://github.com/jdoe/exoplanet-survey . Can you set it up?
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/jdoe/exoplanet-survey right away without asking where to put it or what working directory/folder to use, asks for a database name (since none was given), verifies the template structure, sets db_name accordingly, and only in the final wrap-up tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for next steps.

assertions.text: Does not ask the user where to clone the repo, what directory to use, or what their working directory should be before running git clone
Clones https://github.com/jdoe/exoplanet-survey
Asks the user for a database name, since none was provided in the prompt
In the wrap-up (after cloning), tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for subsequent steps
